title: C6-MT022-BottomSearchRailScopes

scope.summary:
Goal and expected diff shape:
Add a persistent bottom search rail to the native shell. The rail sits in a fixed horizontal strip at the very bottom of the main window frame, BELOW the docked tile area and ABOVE the OS task bar -- it is always visible and never collapses into a drawer. Its sole job is query input + scope selection. Results are NOT rendered inside the rail; the rail dispatches a search intent to the active pane or opens a floating results overlay (MT-023 and later MTs may surface results in the bottom drawer stash shelf, but the rail itself only emits intents). The rail is entirely separate from the bottom drawer stash shelf (MT-023); the rail is a search bar, the drawer is a content shelf.

The rail contains from left to right: (1) a small scope selector pill/chip strip showing all nine fixed scopes -- `project:` `file:` `pane:` `window:` `stash:` `trace:` `terminal:` `stage:` `layout:` -- each rendered as a small toggle button; the active scope is highlighted; (2) a text input field that accepts a free-text query plus the operator-prefix syntax already defined in the legacy parser (`tag:`, `mention:`, `kind:`, `path:`) allowing inline scope narrowing via typed prefixes in addition to the pill toggle; (3) a small clear/cancel button (`x`) that clears the input and deactivates any scope override; (4) an optional `Loom` icon button shortcut that forces the query into the `project:` scope and fires the backend loom-search-v2 (`POST /workspaces/:workspace_id/loom/search-v2`) -- this is the most-used search path and should be one click from any surface.

Exact files to create or edit (all under `src/frontend/`):
- CREATE `src/frontend/search/bottom_rail.rs` -- the `BottomSearchRail` widget struct plus `BottomSearchRail::show(&mut self, ui: &mut egui::Ui, app_state: &mut AppState)` method. Holds: `query: String`, `active_scope: SearchScope`, `pending_result: Option<SearchResult>`, `input_id: egui::Id`.
- CREATE `src/frontend/search/scope.rs` -- the `SearchScope` enum with the nine variants: `Project`, `File`, `Pane`, `Window`, `Stash`, `Trace`, `Terminal`, `Stage`, `Layout`. Impl `Display` returning the colon-suffixed label string (`"project:"` etc.). Impl `SearchScope::default() -> Self { SearchScope::Project }`.
- CREATE `src/frontend/search/mod.rs` -- re-exports `BottomSearchRail`, `SearchScope`, `parse_rail_query`.
- CREATE `src/frontend/search/query_parser.rs` -- the `parse_rail_query(raw: &str, scope: SearchScope) -> RailQuery` function. `RailQuery` holds `free_text: String`, `scope: SearchScope`, `tag_ids: Vec<String>`, `path: Option<String>`, `kind_filters: Vec<String>`. Port the tokenization and operator-prefix logic from `app/src/lib/loom_search_query.ts` (`parseLoomSearchOperators`) to Rust: same regex pattern `^([a-zA-Z_][a-zA-Z0-9_]*):(.*)$`, same quoted-token splitting, same `tag:`, `mention:`, `path:`/`folder:`, `kind:` operators, plus the nine scope-prefix operators as aliases for the scope enum. When the raw query begins with `project:` or any of the nine scope prefixes, override `scope` from the typed prefix and strip the prefix from `free_text`.
- EDIT `src/frontend/shell/mod.rs` (or the main shell layout file, wherever the window's bottom strip is composed) to allocate a `ui.horizontal(...)` strip using `egui::TopBottomPanel::bottom("hsk.bottom_rail")` with a fixed height of 32px and call `BottomSearchRail::show(...)` inside it.

Exact crates and APIs to use:
- `egui::TopBottomPanel::bottom(id)` with `.exact_height(32.0)` to pin the rail at the bottom.
- `egui::TextEdit::singleline(&mut self.query)` with `.desired_width(f32::INFINITY)` (fill remaining space) for the input field.
- `egui::Button` for each scope pill and the clear/Loom shortcut buttons.
- `egui::Id::new("hsk.bottom_rail.input")` as the stable focus id for the text input.
- Do NOT use any external HTTP client inside this MT; the rail only constructs and emits `RailQuery` structs. The actual backend calls (`POST /workspaces/:workspace_id/loom/search-v2`, `GET /workspaces/:workspace_id/loom/graph-search`) will be wired by the pane/overlay that consumes `RailQuery` events. For this MT, emit the query via an `Arc<Mutex<Option<RailQuery>>>` slot on `AppState` that downstream panes can poll.

Step-by-step implementation notes:
1. Create `src/frontend/search/scope.rs` with the enum and Display impl.
2. Create `src/frontend/search/query_parser.rs`. Translate `splitQueryTokens` to an iterator over Rust `&str` slices, handling quoted tokens with a state machine (same double-quote escaping as the TS source). Translate `parseLoomSearchOperators` operator dispatch logic. Add scope-prefix detection: if any token is literally one of the nine scope strings (`project:` `file:` etc.) with an empty operand, override `scope` and remove that token from free-text.
3. Create `src/frontend/search/bottom_rail.rs`:
   a. `show()` opens `egui::TopBottomPanel::bottom("hsk.bottom_rail").exact_height(32.0)` inside the frame's central panel pass -- the panel must be registered before `CentralPanel` so it claims its space first.
   b. Render the nine scope pills left-to-right: `for scope in SearchScope::all() { if ui.selectable_label(self.active_scope == scope, scope.to_string()).clicked() { self.active_scope = scope; } }`.
   c. Render the text input. On `Enter` key or on the `Loom` button click, call `parse_rail_query(&self.query, self.active_scope)` and write the result into `app_state.search_rail_query.lock().unwrap().replace(rq)`.
   d. Render the `x` clear button: clear `self.query`, reset scope to default, clear `app_state.search_rail_query`.
   e. Assign AccessKit id `"hsk.bottom_rail.input"` (Role::TextInput, actions: [Action::Focus, Action::SetValue]) to the text input widget. Assign `"hsk.bottom_rail.scope.{scope_name}"` (Role::Button / ToggleButton, actions: [Action::Click]) to each pill. Assign `"hsk.bottom_rail.clear"` and `"hsk.bottom_rail.loom"` (Role::Button, actions: [Action::Click]) to the auxiliary buttons.
4. Edit the shell layout file: add `use crate::search::BottomSearchRail;`, store `bottom_rail: BottomSearchRail` on the `App` struct, call `self.bottom_rail.show(ctx, &mut self.app_state)` at the START of `eframe::App::update()` so the panel is registered before `CentralPanel`.
5. In `AppState` (wherever it is declared), add `search_rail_query: Arc<Mutex<Option<RailQuery>>>` if it doesn't exist.

Dependencies and unblock conditions:
- MT-001 (toolkit spike): must be complete so `egui` panel APIs are confirmed.
- MT-002 (shell skeleton): must exist so there is a `src/frontend/shell/mod.rs` and an `App` struct to attach the panel and the `AppState` field.
- MT-005 (AppState / IPC bridge): must exist so `AppState` is declared and the `search_rail_query` slot can be added without conflict.

Risk if missed:
- MT-023 (bottom drawer stash shelf) shows drawer items triggered from stash-scope searches; without the rail, MT-023 has no input surface.
- MT-024 (drawer item actions) references items surfaced via the rail/drawer pipeline; it is also unblocked by the rail.
- MT-025 (AccessKit tree) requires all interactive widgets from C1-C6 to already be registered; the rail's nine pills and text input are C6 interactive widgets that MT-025 must annotate.
acceptance_criteria: AC-022-1: The bottom search rail is rendered as a fixed 32px horizontal strip at the bottom of the shell window on every frame; it never collapses or scrolls out of view.

AC-022-2: All nine scope pills (`project:` `file:` `pane:` `window:` `stash:` `trace:` `terminal:` `stage:` `layout:`) are visible and selectable; the active scope pill is visually distinct from inactive ones (e.g. filled vs ghost background).
AC-022-3: Clicking a scope pill sets the active scope; it does not fire a search -- only the Enter key or the Loom button fires a search intent.
AC-022-4: Typing a scope prefix in the text input (e.g. `project:foo`) overrides the active scope to match the typed prefix and removes the prefix from the free-text portion of the parsed query.

AC-022-5: Pressing Enter or clicking the Loom shortcut button calls `parse_rail_query` and writes the resulting `RailQuery` into `app_state.search_rail_query`; the written struct has `free_text` and `scope` correctly set from the input.
AC-022-6: Clicking the `x` clear button empties the input field, resets the active scope to `Project`, and sets `app_state.search_rail_query` to `None`.

AC-022-7: All rail interactive widgets have stable AccessKit `author_id` strings, correct `Role`, and at least one declared `Action` (verified by the `assert_no_unnamed_interactive` check from MT-025 or a local inline assertion in the rail's show() method).

AC-022-8: `parse_rail_query` unit tests pass: free-text extraction, tag/mention/path/kind operator parsing, scope-prefix override, quoted-token splitting. Tests must cover at least the same cases as `app/src/lib/loom_search_query.test.ts`.
AC-022-9: The rail does NOT make any HTTP or IPC backend call itself; it only writes a `RailQuery` to the AppState slot.
AC-022-10: `cargo build` (debug) succeeds with no compile errors for the `src/frontend` crate after this MT.
proof_targets: PROOF-022-1 (unit test): `cargo test -p frontend search::query_parser` passes with tests covering: (a) free-text with no operators, (b) tag+mention+path+kind operators combined, (c) scope-prefix override via typed `project:`, (d) quoted token with spaces, (e) invalid `kind:` value is captured in an error field without panicking. Output: all tests green.

PROOF-022-2 (kittest / visual): Run the egui kittest harness against a minimal frame that includes `BottomSearchRail::show()`. Assert: (a) 9 scope pill buttons render with correct label text; (b) clicking `file:` pill changes `active_scope` to `SearchScope::File`; (c) typing `project:hello world` in the text input and pressing Enter results in `app_state.search_rail_query` containing `RailQuery { scope: Project, free_text: "hello world", ... }`. Output: kittest assertions pass.

PROOF-022-3 (AccessKit snapshot): Run `cargo test -p frontend test_accesskit_bottom_rail` (or the analogous kittest AccessKit tree dump). Assert: every widget registered by `BottomSearchRail::show()` appears in the AccessKit node tree with a non-empty `name` and a non-None `NodeId`. Output: zero unnamed interactive widgets in the bottom rail subtree.

PROOF-022-4 (build): `cargo build --bin handshake_native` (or equivalent binary target) completes with zero errors and zero `unused import` warnings in `src/frontend/search/**`. Output: clean build log.
implementation_notes: The rail uses `egui::TopBottomPanel::bottom("hsk.bottom_rail")` -- this MUST be called before `egui::CentralPanel::default()` within the same `eframe::App::update()` call or egui will panic about panel ordering.

The nine scope variants map to these colon-label strings: Project->"project:", File->"file:", Pane->"pane:", Window->"window:", Stash->"stash:", Trace->"trace:", Terminal->"terminal:", Stage->"stage:", Layout->"layout:". Implement `SearchScope::all() -> &'static [SearchScope]` returning them in this order.

The scope pill strip may overflow the rail width on very small windows. Use `egui::ScrollArea::horizontal()` wrapping the pill strip so pills do not clip the text input. The text input must always be visible even when pills scroll.
The `RailQuery` struct should derive `Clone, Debug, PartialEq`. Keep it in `src/frontend/search/query_parser.rs`.

The `loom_search_query.ts` operator pattern `^([a-zA-Z_][a-zA-Z0-9_]*):(.*)$` in Rust becomes `Regex::new(r"^([a-zA-Z_][a-zA-Z0-9_]*):(.*)")` from the `regex` crate, or use a hand-rolled `str::split_once(':')` with a manual alphanumeric prefix check to avoid the regex dependency -- prefer the no-regex approach for compile speed.

The nine scope prefixes (`project`, `file`, `pane`, `window`, `stash`, `trace`, `terminal`, `stage`, `layout`) should be handled as keyword matches AFTER the standard operator set (`tag`, `mention`, `path`, `folder`, `kind`) in the parser. If a token matches a scope keyword with an empty operand (i.e. just `project:` with nothing after), treat it as a scope selector token and remove it from free-text.

AccessKit integration: call `let ak_id = registry.get_or_insert("hsk.bottom_rail.input")` and push a node with `Role::TextInput` and actions `[Action::Focus, Action::SetValue]`. Do this inline in `show()` using whatever AccessKit helper the MT-001 spike established. If MT-025 is not yet complete, add a `#[cfg(debug_assertions)] assert!(!key.is_empty())` guard inline.

Do NOT make backend search calls inside this MT. The rail emits `RailQuery` only. Backend wiring (calling `POST /workspaces/:workspace_id/loom/search-v2`) belongs to the pane or overlay that consumes the rail's output, which is out of scope for this MT.
ports_from_react: app/src/lib/loom_search_query.ts -- operator-prefix parsing logic (`parseLoomSearchOperators`, `splitQueryTokens`, `OPERATOR_PATTERN`, `splitOperatorValues`). Port entire logic to `src/frontend/search/query_parser.rs`.
app/src/lib/loom_search_query.test.ts -- test cases. Port to `tests/native_gui/test_search_query_parser.rs` or inline in `src/frontend/search/query_parser.rs` as `#[cfg(test)]` module.

app/src/components/LoomSearchV2Panel.tsx -- reference for the search operator UX pattern (query input + Enter to search + scope/facet selection) and the backend endpoint used (`loomSearchV2` -> `POST /workspaces/:workspace_id/loom/search-v2`).
app/src/components/QuickSwitcher.tsx -- reference for the source-kind scope label set (`QUICK_SWITCHER_SOURCE_KINDS`, `SOURCE_LABELS`) which overlap with the nine rail scopes.
binds_backend_api: None -- this MT does NOT make backend calls. It only produces a `RailQuery` struct. The downstream consumer (pane overlay) will call POST /workspaces/:workspace_id/loom/search-v2 (backend: `handshake_core src/api/loom.rs` route `loom_search_v2`) and/or GET /workspaces/:workspace_id/loom/graph-search (backend route `search_loom_graph`).

hbr_obligations: HBR-VIS: all nine scope pills and the text input must be AccessKit-annotated so the swarm vision layer can read and toggle them without screen-reading heuristics.

HBR-SWARM: the `app_state.search_rail_query` slot must be safe to read from a concurrent swarm agent thread (wrap in `Arc<Mutex<...>>` or use an `Arc<RwLock<...>>` if reads dominate); no deadlock between the UI thread writer and potential agent reader.
HBR-QUIET: the rail must not pop any foreground dialog, notification, or focus-stealing element when a search intent is emitted -- only write to the shared slot silently.
HBR-INT: the `parse_rail_query` function must have unit tests that pass in CI (`cargo test`) before the MT is declared complete.

risks: RISK-022-A: Panel ordering panic -- egui panics at runtime if `TopBottomPanel::bottom` is registered AFTER `CentralPanel::default` in the same update() call. A distracted coder puts the rail call after the central panel.
RISK-022-B: Scope-pill overflow clips the text input on small windows or when the window is narrow (e.g. on a 13" laptop). The input becomes inaccessible.
RISK-022-C: The typed scope-prefix override (`project:foo`) and the pill selector conflict: coder implements only one and ignores the other, breaking one of the two AC.
RISK-022-D: The `RailQuery` slot on AppState is not wrapped in a mutex/rwlock, causing a data race when a swarm agent reads concurrently with the UI thread writing.
RISK-022-E: The Rust regex crate is added as a heavy dependency causing long compile times; should be replaced with a lightweight hand-rolled split as noted in implementation notes.
RISK-022-F: AccessKit ids are assigned with ad-hoc per-frame `egui::Id` counters instead of stable string keys, making them non-deterministic across frames and breaking swarm addressing.
minimum_controls: CONTROL-022-A: AC-022-10 (cargo build passes) will catch the panel ordering panic immediately at first cargo build run; the coder MUST build and run before marking the MT complete.

CONTROL-022-B: The ScrollArea wrapping the pill strip (noted in implementation notes) is the control; PROOF-022-2 kittest must simulate a narrow-window scenario and assert the text input is still reachable.
CONTROL-022-C: AC-022-3 and AC-022-4 cover both pill and typed-prefix scope selection independently; both ACs must pass in PROOF-022-2.

CONTROL-022-D: The implementation note mandates `Arc<Mutex<...>>` or `Arc<RwLock<...>>`; the validator must grep the AppState definition for the slot type and reject if it is a bare `Option<RailQuery>`.
CONTROL-022-E: Implementation notes explicitly say prefer no-regex; the validator checks the `Cargo.toml` for a `regex` dependency added by this MT and raises a flag if present.
CONTROL-022-F: PROOF-022-3 (AccessKit snapshot test) verifies non-empty NodeIds; MT-025 `assert_no_unnamed_interactive` is the systemic gate.

wp_validator_review.reviewer: INTEGRATION_VALIDATOR(acting WP_VALIDATOR) session boa3epvo9 — operator-directed retro WP-Validator pass
wp_validator_review.method: deep code review + adversarial verify vs MT/WP/Master-Spec intent (governance paperwork non-blocking per operator)
wp_validator_review.one_line: Independently confirmed PASS: the bottom search rail honestly implements input + nine scope pills + operator-prefix parser + intent emission into a real lock-guarded Arc<Mutex> slot with stable AccessKit identities, proven end-to-end through the actual HandshakeApp via egui_kittest; search EXECUTION is the contract's own out-of-scope boundary (binds_backend_api None), not a deferred-live escape, and the parser parity micro-gaps I found are cosmetic and not load-bearing for any AC.

acceptance_summary.criterion: AC-022-1: 32px always-visible bottom strip, never collapses
acceptance_summary.evidence: app.rs drive_search_rail (1663) uses egui::TopBottomPanel::bottom("handshake_bottom_search_rail").exact_height(RAIL_HEIGHT=32.0). Independently verified call order: status bar at app.rs:4045, drive_search_rail at 4068, drawer at 4071+, CentralPanel at 4227 — rail registered BEFORE CentralPanel so it claims its space first; no panel-ordering panic (RISK-022-A). RAIL_HEIGHT=32.0 const at search_rail.rs:70. The pinned panel is unconditional every frame (no collapse path).

acceptance_summary.criterion: AC-022-2: nine pills visible/selectable, active visually distinct
acceptance_summary.evidence: search_rail.rs scope_pill() (576) fills selected pill with colors.active_pill_bg vs inactive_pill_bg and sets .selected(selected) + node.set_selected(true). SearchScope::all() (106) returns exactly 9 in fixed order. test_accesskit_ids.rs live-frame snapshot asserts exactly the 9 bottom-rail.scope.* Button nodes present (count 67->79), and test_search_rail.rs rail_renders_pills_input_and_buttons_live asserts pills.len()==9 in a real frame.

acceptance_summary.criterion: AC-022-3: pill click sets scope, only Enter/Loom fires
acceptance_summary.evidence: search_rail.rs:489-492 pill click sets state.active_scope only (no fire). Fire set true only on Enter (line 539-541) or loom (546-549). kittest clicking_file_pill_sets_scope_without_emitting asserts search_rail_query() is None after a pill click, then proves a subsequent Enter emits with the File scope.

acceptance_summary.criterion: AC-022-4: typed scope prefix overrides + strips from free-text
acceptance_summary.evidence: parse_rail_query lines 285-293: SearchScope::from_keyword override wins, residual value kept as free-text. Unit test parse_scope_prefix_override_with_residual_free_text + kittest typed_scope_prefix_overrides_in_emitted_intent (project:hello world with File pill -> scope=Project, free_text="hello world").

acceptance_summary.criterion: AC-022-5: Enter/Loom writes parsed RailQuery into app_state.search_rail_query
acceptance_summary.evidence: app.rs drive_search_rail RailOutcome::Fire writes Some(*query) into self.search_rail_query lock (1700+). kittest asserts the emitted intent has correct scope+free_text read back through the REAL HandshakeApp via search_rail_query()/search_rail_query_slot() off the same lock. Minor: Fire is gated on has_workspace (non-blocking, see concerns).

acceptance_summary.criterion: AC-022-6: clear empties input, resets scope to Project, slot=None
acceptance_summary.evidence: search_rail.rs:555-558 clear resets state.query + state.active_scope to default and emits RailOutcome::Clear; app.rs writes None into slot. kittest clear_button_resets_input_scope_and_slot asserts None after clear, then a fresh bare-query emit carries Project scope (proving the scope reset).

acceptance_summary.criterion: AC-022-7: stable AccessKit author_id + Role + Action on all rail widgets
acceptance_summary.evidence: 3 fixed controls (RAIL_INPUT/CLEAR/LOOM, NodeIds 22/23/24) enumerated in DECLARED_IDENTITIES (registry.rs:438-461). Input uses emit_interactive_node (live.rs:128) which preserves egui's native TextInput role + Focus/SetValue actions and adds the stable author_id; clear/loom use fixed_id_button with set_role(Button)+set_author_id; 9 pills carry bottom-rail.scope.{kw} author_ids with Role::Button. test_accesskit_ids.rs live-frame snapshot asserts input=TextInput and all rest=Button; the systemic MT-025 assert_no_unnamed_interactive gate runs over the live tree (count 79).

acceptance_summary.criterion: AC-022-8: parser unit tests cover PROOF-022-1 cases incl. parity with loom_search_query.test.ts
acceptance_summary.evidence: search_rail.rs #[cfg(test)] (651+) covers free-text, combined tag/mention/path/kind, scope-prefix override, bare scope selector, quoted token with spaces, invalid kind captured-not-panicked, unknown operator passthrough, empty input. Faithful port of splitQueryTokens/splitOperatorValues/OPERATOR_PATTERN (no-regex split_operator, search_rail.rs:364). NOTE: the kind vocabulary is the backend LoomBlockContentType set, not the React LoomGraphSearchSourceKind set, so the literal React kind:document/kind:loom_block cases map differently — defensible backend-aligned mapping, PROOF-022-1e behavior holds (see concerns).

acceptance_summary.criterion: AC-022-9: rail makes NO backend/HTTP/IPC call
acceptance_summary.evidence: Independent grep of search_rail.rs for reqwest/http/post/get/backend_client/runtime_handle/send/url/client returns ONLY a doc-comment match (line 14); no backend import, no Cargo.toml change. The rail only constructs RailQuery and writes the slot. Commit note + module docs record the first build wrongly called /loom/search-v2 and was re-scoped to intent-emission.

Validator ground truth (relied upon per instructions): handshake_native crate + all test binaries compile clean (cargo build --tests exit 0); curated deterministic suite green including test_search_rail and test_accesskit_ids. No regex dependency added (CONTROL-022-E satisfied).

spec_realism.detail: binds_backend_api is explicitly None for this MT and the contract repeatedly scopes search EXECUTION and results display OUT ('Do NOT make backend search calls inside this MT'; 'Backend wiring ... belongs to the pane or overlay that consumes the rail's output'). The 'deferred to a downstream consumer' phrasing is the contract's own out-of-scope boundary, not an escape hiding required behavior. The rail's required job — input, scope selection, operator-prefix parse, and emitting a RailQuery into Arc<Mutex<Option<RailQuery>>> — is fully implemented; I grepped the touched code + tests and found NO todo!/unimplemented!/stub/early-stub-return/'not yet wired'/'pending MT-'/...Unavailable on the behavior path. The Handshake-owned resource exercised here is the AccessKit live node tree, and the proof is REAL not mock-only: test_search_rail.rs drives the actual HandshakeApp through egui_kittest (focus input, type_text, key_press Enter, click pills/clear/Loom) and asserts the emitted intent + slot state off the SAME Arc lock; the slot is a genuine shared Arc (test rail_query_slot_shared_across_clones); test_accesskit_ids.rs dumps the real live frame and asserts exactly 12 rail nodes with correct roles. No key integration test is #[ignore]'d to hide unproven behavior. Spec-Realism sub-rule 3 (implementer self-certification) is satisfied by THIS independent review per operator directive.

concerns_non_blocking: File layout deviates from the contract's prescribed src/frontend/search/ module (scope.rs, query_parser.rs, bottom_rail.rs, mod.rs) and from the allowed_paths globs (src/frontend/search/**, src/frontend/shell/**). The coder consolidated everything into the real native crate at src/frontend/handshake_native/src/search_rail.rs and integrated into app.rs (the actual shell). All required types (SearchScope, RailQuery, parse_rail_query, show()) exist and are exported via lib.rs. Structure-only divergence; behavior is equivalent. Worth a note since the allowed_paths in the contract do not literally match the worktree's crate layout.

kind: validation vocabulary is the backend LoomBlockContentType set (note/file/annotated_file/tag_hub/journal/canvas/view_def, matching migrations 0013/0334/0335) rather than the React LoomGraphSearchSourceKind set (loom_block/document/symbol/work_packet/...). Consequence: the literal React test cases (kind:document, kind:loom_block) would land in kind_errors under the Rust port. This is a defensible mapping toward the search-v2 content_type contract the downstream consumer will actually filter on; the required PROOF-022-1e behavior (valid kept, invalid captured in kind_errors, no panic) holds. Confirm the downstream consumer (MT-023+) expects this content_type vocabulary.

Three React parser parity micro-gaps NOT ported, none asserted by any AC or load-bearing for intent emission: (1) leading-# strip on tag values (React value.replace(/^#/,'') -> tag:#x yields 'x'; Rust keeps '#x'); (2) unique() dedup of tag/mention/kind (React dedups; Rust keeps duplicates); (3) split_operator_values splits on comma AND whitespace+tab (Rust) vs comma-only (React), so a quoted multi-word operator value like tag:"a b" would split into ["a","b"] in Rust vs ["a b"] in React. Cosmetic vs the TS source; a downstream consumer can normalize. Not a code FAIL.

Fire is gated on has_workspace (!active_project_id.is_empty()) in app.rs drive_search_rail; AC-022-5 imposes no such gate. Harmless because active_project_id defaults to non-empty DEFAULT_PROJECT_ID ('default-project') in both constructors (app.rs:719, 891) and in every test, so the slot is always written. A future genuinely-empty-workspace state would silently swallow a fire — minor latent edge case.

No explicit narrow-window kittest asserting the input stays reachable (PROOF-022-2b / CONTROL-022-B). Mitigated structurally: the input+clear+Loom sit in a right_to_left group with desired_width(INFINITY), so egui reserves the input before the pills can overflow, and the coder deliberately avoided a horizontal ScrollArea (which would add an anonymous interactive node tripping the MT-025 gate). The structural guarantee is sound and the input's live reachability is proven in the default frame, but the specific narrow-window proof target is not directly exercised.

Markdown-projection / paperwork fields in the MT contract remain PENDING (lifecycle.status PENDING, validator_verdict PENDING, handoff sessions null). Per operator directive these are NOT blockers; this independent review IS the certification (claimed_by == ready_for_validation_by is acceptable for this folded WP).
